verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I single-pass TM p24-family cargo receptor predominantly resident in ER-Golgi cisternae cycling through the early secretory pathway. NCBI explicitly notes plasma membrane localization in addition to Golgi, consistent with a documented minority PM pool from secretory cycling. The protein has its own TM anchor with a small luminal/extracellular GOLD domain that would face outside at the PM. Surface pool is minor relative to dominant endomembrane residence, so `dual_localization` under `contextual` rather than `yes`.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Unclear whether the PM pool is substantial/persistent enough for binder accessibility or merely transient transit; no known cell-surface-directed clinical program.
